articles.description: NASA's Artemis II mission has traveled farther from Earth than any mission before. Here's how to watch the crew's return landing in the Pacific Ocean.

articles.title: Anthropic temporarily banned OpenClaw’s creator from accessing Claude
articles.description: This ban took place after Claude's pricing changed for OpenClaw users last week.

articles.title: Battery recycler Ascend Elements files for bankruptcy
articles.description: Ascend Elements said that it will be filing for Chapter 11 bankruptcy in the wake of a canceled government grant and a challenging market for lithium-ion batteries.

articles.title: Stalking victim sues OpenAI, claims ChatGPT fueled her abuser’s delusions and ignored her warnings
articles.description: OpenAI ignored three warnings that a ChatGPT user was dangerous — including its own mass-casualty flag — while he stalked and harassed his ex-girlfriend, a new lawsuit alleges.

articles.title: TechCrunch is heading to Tokyo — and bringing the Startup Battlefield with it
articles.description: SusHi Tech 2026 is zeroing in on four technology domains reshaping society: AI, Robotics, Resilience, and Entertainment. Expect live demos of humanoid robots, sessions on autonomous driving's software revolution, deep dives into cyber defense and climate tech, and candid conversations about how AI i

articles.title: France to ditch Windows for Linux to reduce reliance on US tech
articles.description: France's move to ditch Windows for Linux is its latest effort to reduce its reliance on American tech giants.

articles.title: YouTube Premium and YouTube Music are getting more expensive
articles.description: The YouTube Premium individual plan is increasing from $13.99 to $15.99 per month, while the family plan is increasing from $22.99 to $26.99 per month.

articles.title: Every fusion startup that has raised over $100M
articles.description: Fusion startups have raised $7.1 billion to date, with the majority of it going to a handful of companies.

articles.title: Last 24 hours: Save up to $500 on your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass
articles.description: This is it. Tonight is your last chance to lock in savings of up to $500 for your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass. These discounts end at 11:59 p.m. PT.

articles.title: PSA: If you use the Meta AI app, your friends will find out and it will be embarrassing
articles.description: Now that Meta released its Muse Spark model, more people are downloading the Meta AI app. And if you join the app, your friends will probably get an Instagram notification about it.

articles.title: Snap gets closer to releasing new AI glasses after years-long hiatus
articles.description: Specs has long been teasing the next version of its augmented reality glasses. A new partnership with Qualcomm promises some movement in that department.

digest:
## 🔬 Major Technical Developments

### AI assistance when contributing to the Linux kernel
URL: https://github.com/torvalds/linux/blob/master/Documentation/process/coding-assistants.rst
Official kernel documentation now addresses AI-assisted contributions.
The Linux kernel maintainers have formalized guidance on using coding assistants, signaling institutional acceptance of AI tooling in critical infrastructure development. This is notable because kernel contributions face extreme scrutiny—if AI passes muster here, it validates the tooling for enterprise adoption broadly.

### WireGuard makes new Windows release following Microsoft signing resolution
URL: https://lists.zx2c4.com/pipermail/wireguard/2026-April/009561.html
Microsoft code signing friction resolved, unblocking WireGuard's Windows distribution.
Enterprise VPN infrastructure depends on smooth Windows deployment. The signing resolution removes a material barrier to WireGuard adoption in corporate environments where it competes with legacy solutions from Cisco and Palo Alto. Watch for accelerated enterprise migration.

### CPU-Z and HWMonitor compromised
URL: https://www.theregister.com/2026/04/10/cpuid_site_hijacked/
CPUID's site hijacked, distributing compromised versions of widely-used system utilities.
These tools are installed on millions of enthusiast and enterprise machines for hardware diagnostics. Supply chain attacks on trusted utilities represent a growing attack surface—this follows the pattern of Codecov, SolarWinds, and similar incidents. Organizations running these tools need immediate remediation.

## 💼 Business & Market Signals

### France to ditch Windows for Linux to reduce reliance on US tech
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/10/france-to-ditch-windows-for-linux-to-reduce-reliance-on-us-tech/
Sovereign tech strategy drives government migration away from Microsoft.
This is the clearest signal yet of European digital sovereignty mandates creating real procurement shifts. Microsoft faces headwinds in EU government contracts; Red Hat, SUSE, and European Linux consultancies are positioned beneficiaries. The knock-on effects for Azure adoption in French public sector are significant.

### Battery recycler Ascend Elements files for bankruptcy
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/10/battery-recycler-ascend-elements-files-for-bankruptcy/
Chapter 11 filing follows canceled government grant amid weak lithium-ion market.
Ascend raised over $700M and was positioned as the domestic champion for battery recycling. The combination of policy uncertainty (grant cancellation) and commodity price pressure (lithium down 80% from peak) creates distress across the battery value chain. This is a special situations opportunity—watch for asset sales.

### Anthropic temporarily banned OpenClaw's creator from accessing Claude
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/10/anthropic-temporarily-banned-openclaws-creator-from-accessing-claude/
Platform risk materializes for developers building on AI APIs.
Following Claude's pricing changes affecting OpenClaw users, this ban highlights the precarious position of third-party developers in the AI stack. Anthropic is demonstrating willingness to enforce terms aggressively. Developers building AI wrappers face existential platform risk—this is the Twitter API playbook repeating.

### Every fusion startup that has raised over $100M
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/10/every-fusion-startup-that-has-raised-over-100m/
$7.1B deployed into fusion with concentration in a handful of players.
Capital intensity and timeline uncertainty make fusion a challenging PE category, but the sector is entering a phase where technical milestones (net energy gain demonstrated) are validating the thesis. Commonwealth Fusion, Helion, and TAE remain the category leaders. Secondary opportunities may emerge as smaller players seek exits.

## 📋 Case Studies & Deep Dives

### Selective Test Execution at Stripe: Fast CI for a 50M-line Ruby monorepo
URL: https://stripe.dev/blog/selective-test-execution-at-stripe-fast-ci-for-a-50m-line-ruby-monorepo
Stripe's engineering team details how they run CI on a massive Ruby codebase.
At 50M lines, Stripe's monorepo is among the largest Ruby deployments anywhere. Their selective execution approach—running only tests affected by changes—is a blueprint for enterprises struggling with CI costs and developer velocity. This validates the monorepo strategy at scale and offers replicable patterns.

### Helium is hard to replace
URL: https://www.construction-physics.com/p/helium-is-hard-to-replace
Strategic commodity analysis on helium's irreplaceable role in semiconductors, MRI, and aerospace.
Helium is non-substitutable for chip fab cooling and superconducting applications. Supply comes from a handful of sources (US Federal Reserve depleting, Qatar dominant). Any PE thesis in semiconductor equipment, medical imaging, or cryogenics needs to model helium supply risk. This is a quiet but material constraint on fab expansion.

### Ursa - a new Iceberg-first storage engine for Kafka
URL: https://topicpartition.io/blog/ursa-a-new-lakehouse-first-storage-engine-for-kafka
Kafka gets a native Iceberg storage layer, bridging streaming and lakehouse.
The data infrastructure stack is converging: Kafka for streaming, Iceberg for analytics. Ursa eliminates the connector layer, reducing complexity and latency. Confluent should be watching—this is the open-source pressure pattern that erodes commercial moats. Databricks and Snowflake interoperability implications are significant.

## ⚡ Signal vs. Noise

The week's signal is clear: platform control is tightening across the stack. France pushing Linux, Anthropic banning developers, Microsoft signing friction—these are all manifestations of the same dynamic: infrastructure providers asserting control, and users (governments, developers, enterprises) discovering their dependencies. The Ascend bankruptcy is the flip side: policy-dependent businesses face binary outcomes when the political winds shift. For special situations, the play is finding quality assets caught in these control transitions—distressed but not broken.
